change_records.title: MR-003: Static lapse table replaced by calibrated dynamic lapse
change_records.description: Phase 13 Task 2: Implemented an interest-rate-dependent dynamic lapse function (duration base x bounded arctan efficiency multiplier + logistic rate-induced mass lapse) and calibrated it to a synthetic HK PAR endowment lapse experience study. Resolves the static-lapse limitation MR-003 that rendered TVOG lapse sensitivity FLAT. assumption="dynamic_lapse".

after_snapshot.lapse_model: Blended duration base (Opt C) x bounded arctan efficiency multiplier (Opt A) + logistic rate-induced mass lapse (Opt B)

change_records.impact_assessment: Dynamic lapse raises surrenders when market rates exceed the credited rate and lowers them when the guarantee is in-the-money, materially changing PV of surrender benefits and net liability under rate stress. Lapse sensitivity is now non-FLAT, enabling meaningful TVOG and ALM behaviour.
change_records.quantitative_impact: At +400 bps shock: PV net liability moves -95.75% vs static baseline (static-lapse sensitivity was FLAT). Calibration R2=0.9999, RMSE=0.00062.
change_records.author: AutomatedModelDev_Phase13

sign_off_history.comments: Dynamic lapse implemented and calibrated; submitting for peer review.
Functional form and calibration diagnostics reviewed; fit within tolerance.
Dynamic lapse assumption approved for educational use; production use requires credible experience study and independent review.

change_records.phase: Phase 13: Production Readiness and Live Market Integration

change_records.title: MR-001: Default discount rate reduced 3.5% -> 3.0% (CBIRC cap)
change_records.description: Phase 13 Task 3: Reduced the default liability-reserving discount rate from the legacy 3.5% to 3.0% so the model default complies with the CBIRC 2023 reserve-valuation cap. The 3.5% default breached the cap and understated statutory reserves (critical model risk MR-001). The change updates the defaults of project_liability_cashflows and run_full_projection in monthly_projection.py and the DEFAULT_RESERVING_DISCOUNT_RATE constant. assumption="discount_rate_annual".
change_records.impact_assessment: Lowering the reserving discount rate increases the present value of long-dated guaranteed liabilities, raising statutory reserves and lowering the reported solvency margin — removing the systematic reserve understatement that breached the CBIRC cap. TVOG direction is unchanged but the base TVOG level shifts with the new discount basis. Required by CBIRC C-ROSS for all statutory reserve calculations.
change_records.quantitative_impact: PV net liability for a 20y HK PAR endowment moves +21.65% (PV guaranteed benefits +9.71%) when the discount rate is cut from 3.5% to 3.0%. Impact rises with policy term (longer duration -> greater rate sensitivity).
change_records.author: AutomatedModelDev_Phase13

sign_off_history.comments: Discount-rate change drafted with quantified reserve impact; submitting for peer review.
CBIRC cap breach confirmed; before/after snapshots and reserve impact reviewed and reasonable.
Discount rate 3.0% approved as the reserving default for educational use; production reserving requires a bootstrapped CNY sovereign curve and genuine independent review.

change_records.title: Runtime P/Q measure enforcement in all simulation paths (G-05 / MR-004) — Phase 14 Task 1
change_records.description: Added a runtime measure-enforcement guard (_enforce_simulation_measure) invoked at the entry of every scenario-generation execution path — HullWhiteRateProcess.simulate, G2PlusRateProcess.simulate, GBMEquityProcess.simulate, FXSpotProcess.simulate, and ScenarioSet.generate — plus a post-condition (_assert_output_measure) verifying the output measure stamp. Each generator now declares an explicit SUPPORTED_MEASURES contract. A new MeasureEnforcementError (ValueError subclass) is raised on unsupported or mismatched measures. Closes G-05 at educational level and moves MR-004 to MITIGATED.

before_snapshot.measure_enforcement: Soft coercion (_coerce_measure) at simulate() entry; hard guards only at consumers (RiskMetrics=P, TVOGEngine=Q). No producer-side runtime execution evidence (env lacked numpy/scipy/pandas/pytest).

after_snapshot.measure_enforcement: Runtime guard + SUPPORTED_MEASURES contract + output-stamp post-condition in all 5 generation paths; 30 dedicated runtime tests PASS with genuine execution evidence.

change_records.impact_assessment: Eliminates the residual risk of running a generator under an unintended measure and of silent output mis-stamping. No change to numerical results for valid P/Q requests (reproducibility preserved; verified by existing seed-stability tests).
change_records.author: Automated model-dev cycle (Claude)

change_records.title: MR-002: GBM equity parameters calibrated to market data (CNY + HK)
change_records.description: Phase 14 Task 2: Replaced educational GBM equity placeholders (sigma_S=0.22, ERP=0.045, dividend=0.025, rho=-0.15) with values calibrated from CSI 300 (CNY) and Hang Seng (HK/China) educational-proxy history via GBMCalibrator: blended (60% implied / 40% historical) equity volatility, a historical-excess-return ERP with a 0.7% survivorship adjustment and a 5% cap, an EWMA trailing dividend yield, and a Pearson rate-equity correlation. FileBasedEquitySource is used; production pricing/capital use requires credentialled live extracts and a genuine independent review.
change_records.impact_assessment: Replacing the placeholder GBM parameters with calibrated values reduces the equity risk premium below the 4.5% placeholder (removing the systematic overstatement of investment returns flagged in MR-002), sets sigma_S from a blended historical/implied estimate, and gives a data-based negative rate-equity correlation. This propagates to P-measure VaR/ES, TVOG, and ALM rebalancing.

change_records.title: Phase 14 Task 5: Optional Merton jump-diffusion equity process behind feature flag
change_records.description: Added JumpDiffusionParams + JumpDiffusionEquityProcess (Merton 1976 compound-Poisson jump-diffusion) as an OPTIONAL equity ESG model selectable via the PAR_ESG_EQUITY_MODEL feature flag (default 'gbm', so all existing Phase 4/8 behaviour is byte-for-byte unchanged). The Q-measure jump compensator -lambda*kappa (kappa=exp(mu_J+0.5 sigma_J^2)-1) is applied under both P and Q so the risk-neutral forward E[S(t+dt)/S(t)]=exp((r-q)dt) is preserved exactly, independent of (sigma, lambda, mu_J, sigma_J). Wired equity_model into ScenarioSet.generate and build_equity_process/EQUITY_PROCESS_REGISTRY; jump params recorded in ParameterSnapshot for traceability. Added EquityForwardMartingaleValidator producing reviewable Q-measure equity-forward martingale evidence. Jump parameters are educational PLACEHOLDERS (uncalibrated); production use of the jump model requires calibration to option-implied / historical jump data and independent review.

change_records.title: Phase 18 Task 1 - copula-based tail-dependent risk aggregation (MR-010 mitigation)
change_records.description: Added a copula-based, tail-dependent risk-aggregation engine (par_model_v2/projection/multi_driver_copula_aggregation.py, additive; the variance-covariance ThreeDriverRiskAggregator is left untouched). The engine fits a Gaussian (baseline), a Student-t (symmetric tail dependence) and a survival-Clayton (upper-tail dependence) copula to the realised standalone capital-loss vectors, rebuilds the joint loss from empirical marginals + each copula, reads the 99.5% aggregate SCR off the simulated joint loss, benchmarks every copula AND the var-covar formula to the three-driver nested ground truth, and selects the best copula by AIC on the pseudo-observations (an empirical-justification step per Solvency II Art. 234, not benchmark fitting).

risk_register.title: Discount rate exceeds CBIRC regulatory cap
risk_register.description: Current discount rate assumption is 3.5%, exceeding the CBIRC regulatory cap of 3.0% (effective 2023 reserve valuation guidance). This produces an understated liability reserve and overstated solvency margin.
risk_register.mitigation: Reduce discount rate to ≤3.0% in Phase 4 calibration; document deviation formally per ASOP 25 §3.3.
risk_register.related_standard: SOA ASOP 25 §3.3; CBIRC Reserve Valuation Guidance 2023
risk_register.notes: Phase 13 Task 3: default discount rate reduced 3.5pct to 3.0pct (CBIRC cap); executed via GovernanceStore ChangeRecord 5dac3fe3 (APPROVED). G-01/G-07 PASS. Production residual: bootstrapped CNY sovereign curve + genuine independent sign-off.

risk_register.title: Investment return assumptions overstated vs CNY market
risk_register.description: Bond return assumptions (4.0–5.0%) are 100–180 bps above current CNY government bond yields (2.2–2.6% as of calibration date). Equity ERP has not been calibrated. This systematically understates TVOG and liability PV.
risk_register.mitigation: Recalibrate bond returns and ERP to CNY market data in Phase 4 (GBMCalibrator); interim sensitivity analysis required.
risk_register.notes: Phase 14 Task 2 (G-03): GBM equity parameters calibrated to CSI 300 (CNY) and Hang Seng (HK/China) educational-proxy history. CNY sigma_S=0.2158, ERP=0.0327 (below the 4.5% placeholder), rho=-0.1965. ChangeRecord APPROVED + one PARAM_CHANGE audit entry per market; G-03 PASS. Production residual: credentialled live extracts + genuine independent APS X2 review.

risk_register.title: Dynamic lapse assumption absent
risk_register.description: No dynamic lapse function is implemented. Dynamic lapse is the single most impactful assumption for TVOG and ALM under stressed rate scenarios. Static lapse at current flat rates will materially understate TVOG sensitivity (estimated ±15–30% TVOG per ±25% lapse shock per Phase 1 assessment).
risk_register.mitigation: Dynamic lapse implemented and calibrated (Phase 13 Task 2); ChangeRecord assumption="dynamic_lapse" APPROVED.
risk_register.notes: Phase 19 Task 5 (G-LAPSE): OU lapse behavioural-index parameters calibrated to HK PAR actual-to-expected lapse-experience educational-proxy history. kappa_b=0.7854 (half-life 0.9yr), long_run_level=-0.0360 (A/E 0.965), sigma_b=0.1781, stationary_std=0.1421. ChangeRecord APPROVED + one PARAM_CHANGE audit entry; G-LAPSE PASS. Residual (keeps the risk open, not closed): single systemic level factor on educational-proxy data; five-driver proxy still omits FX/liquidity; credentialled persistency study + a genuine independent APS X2 review are required.

risk_register.title: P/Q measure not enforced at runtime
risk_register.description: Prior to Phase 2, the codebase did not enforce or document the distinction between real-world (P) and risk-neutral (Q) scenario sets. Mixing measures is a critical actuarial error that invalidates both VaR/ES and TVOG outputs.
risk_register.mitigation: Measure enum implemented in esg_process.py (Phase 2). LossDistribution enforces Measure.P for VaR/ES (risk_metrics.py); TVOGEngine enforces Measure.Q (tvog.py). Phase 14 Task 1: runtime guard _enforce_simulation_measure() now validates the requested measure against each generator's declared SUPPORTED_MEASURES in every simulate()/generate() execution path (HullWhite, G2++, GBM, FX, ScenarioSet), and _assert_output_measure() verifies the output measure stamp. 30 dedicated runtime tests (tests/test_measure_enforcement.py) PASS with genuine runtime execution evidence (numpy/scipy/pandas/pytest available in automation sandbox).
risk_register.notes: Critical Deviation D-04 in MODEL_AUDIT_REPORT.md. Partially remediated. | Phase 14 Task 1 (G-05): runtime measure enforcement added to all simulate()/generate() paths; runtime execution evidence attached (docs/G05_RUNTIME_EVIDENCE_2026-06-04T103044Z.json). Residual: production sign-off requires an independent reviewer to confirm consumer coverage, hence MITIGATED not fully CLOSED.

risk_register.title: Distributed executor pickling failure
risk_register.description: DistributedExecutor fails to serialise complex model objects for multiprocessing, causing 7 test failures in the original repository test suite. This blocks all distributed scenario execution, TVOG computation, and VaR/ES integration.
risk_register.mitigation: RESOLVED — module-level `_execute_task_spec` callable + `make_partial_task` binder; verified by 63 tests.
risk_register.related_standard: SOA ASOP 56 §3.5 (scenario adequacy requires successful batch execution)
risk_register.notes:
Identified in VALIDATION_FRAMEWORK_REVIEW.md as highest-leverage Phase 3 fix.
[2026-06-04] FORMALLY CLOSED (G-10). Bug fixed Phase 3 Task 1 (2026-05-18): Replaced the locally-scoped lambda submitted to the process pool with a module-level `_execute_task_spec(task_spec)` callable plus a `make_partial_task(func, **bound_kwargs)` binder and an explicit `_validate_picklable` guard, so every task object pickles cleanly across the multiprocessing boundary. 63 unit tests confirm correct behaviour (tests/test_distributed_executor.py). Closure verified by Model Developer and approved by Model Owner (ChiefActuary); recorded via GovernanceStore GOVERNANCE audit entry.

risk_register.title: Model validation readiness below production threshold
risk_register.description: Overall validation readiness assessed at 2/5 (development-grade). Stochastic validation (scenario convergence, martingale test, VaR/ES integration) is entirely absent. Model is not fit for regulatory reporting or production use in current state.
risk_register.mitigation: 4-layer validation framework implementation across Phases 3–4. Full validation cadence in Phase 5.
risk_register.related_standard: SOA ASOP 56 §3.5; IA TAS M §3.6; APS X2

risk_register.title: No assumption change control process
risk_register.description: The model has no documented assumption change control process. Any assumption can be changed without sign-off, impact assessment, or audit trail. This is a material IA TAS M §3.7 gap.
risk_register.mitigation: GovernanceStore + ChangeRecord + SignOffWorkflow implemented in Phase 2, Task 4 (this module). Requires organisational adoption.
risk_register.related_standard: IA TAS M §3.7; IA TAS M §3.3
risk_register.notes: Remediated by this module (audit_trail.py). Framework in place; process adoption pending.

risk_register.title: HW1F calibration not yet executed
risk_register.description: HullWhiteCalibrator.calibrate() is a NotImplementedError stub. HW1F parameters (a=0.10, σ_r=0.012) are placeholders producing model swaption vol ~250 bps vs ~42 bps market — a 6x error. All interest rate paths and ZCB prices derived from these parameters are unreliable.

risk_register.notes: Calibration scaffold complete (calibration_framework.py). Data sourcing required.

risk_register.title: Variance-covariance aggregation understates diversified capital
risk_register.description: The standard-formula (variance-covariance) aggregation fed with the raw ESG rate/equity factor correlation understates the fully-diversified nested tail capital by ~33% (29,031 vs 43,251 at 99.5%). The realised capital-loss correlation is +0.55 vs the -0.15 factor correlation because the equity maturity guarantee and rate-driven guaranteed-benefit legs lose value in the same direction under joint down-rate/down-equity stress.
risk_register.mitigation: Benchmark all aggregated figures to the multi-driver nested run; aggregate on the empirical capital-loss correlation rather than the raw ESG factor correlation; document gap in the limitation card. Educational only; not used for any capital decision.
risk_register.related_standard: SOA ASOP 56 §3.5; IA TAS M §3.6; SOA ASOP 25 §3.3
risk_register.notes: Phase 23 Task 4 refresh (WITH management actions): var-covar 14429 vs nested-with 33118 (understatement 56.4%); tail-matched t(2.95) rel err 22.5% vs gaussian 27.8%. The Art.-23 action lowers all aggregates but does NOT close the var-covar tail understatement; t-copula remains the mitigation.

risk_register.title: Multi-driver economic-capital proxy is educational, not production capital
risk_register.description: The Phase 15 multi-driver (short-rate + equity) nested/LSMC economic-capital proxy (Tasks 1-4) is fitted on PLACEHOLDER HW1F / GBM parameters, omits material risk drivers (lapse, mortality/longevity trend, credit spread, FX, liquidity, management action), uses an educational single-guarantee liability, and has NOT been independently reviewed (APS X2). The 99.5% VaR/ES and SCR-proxy figures it produces are illustrative only and must not be used for regulatory or internal economic-capital reporting until parameters are calibrated to credentialled market data, the omitted drivers are brought into the tail, and an independent reviewer signs off.
risk_register.mitigation: Classify all multi-driver capital output as EDUCATIONAL ONLY in the limitation card and at the API boundary; bound proxy error by the Task 2 out-of-sample validation (OOS R^2=0.9704, VaR rel err 3.21%) and outer-sampling error by the Task 4 bootstrap CI (SE ~1.66%); benchmark every aggregated figure to the Task 3 nested ground truth. Residual closure requires credentialled-data calibration + independent APS X2 review (tracked jointly with MR-006 / MR-008).
risk_register.related_standard: IA TAS M §3.6; APS X2 §3; SOA ASOP 56 §3.5
risk_register.notes: Phase 22 Task 3 (G-LIQX): liquidity exposure notional and 7x7 liquidity couplings calibrated — the LAST liquidity placeholders. notional=22000 (reproducible: backing_asset_mv x illiquid_share x forced_sale_fraction); couplings (rate,equity,spread,lapse,mortality,fx)=(-0.079,-0.293,0.458,0.108,0.016,0.107) recovered within +/-0.12 of documented targets from 240 monthly joint observations; 7x7 PSD-validated; var-covar SCR sensitivity bounded. Residual (keeps the risk open): nested/copula re-aggregation with these values is Task 4; documented-targets fixture is educational — credentialled joint data + independent APS X2 review required.

risk_register.title: Credit-spread driver and three-driver economic-capital proxy are educational, not production capital
risk_register.description: Phase 17 adds a CIR++ mean-reverting credit-spread driver (par_model_v2/stochastic/credit_spread.py) as the third risk factor in the nested/LSMC economic-capital proxy (rate + equity + credit-spread), with a reduced-form hazard x LGD credit-loss component on spread-sensitive backing assets. The CIR++ parameters (mean-reversion, long-run spread, vol, risk premium) and the hazard/LGD assumptions are PLACEHOLDER educational defaults, NOT calibrated to credentialled credit-market data; the trivariate LSMC surface still omits material drivers (lapse, mortality/longevity trend, FX, liquidity, management action) and has NOT been independently reviewed (APS X2). The three-driver 99.5% VaR/ES and SCR-proxy figures are illustrative only and must not be used for regulatory or internal economic-capital reporting until the credit parameters are calibrated, the omitted drivers are brought into the tail, and an independent reviewer signs off.
risk_register.mitigation: Classify all three-driver capital output as EDUCATIONAL ONLY in the consolidated limitation card and at the API boundary; bound proxy error by the Task 2 trivariate out-of-sample validation (OOS R^2=0.9751; selected basis deg1/max-int3; leakage-free) and outer-sampling error by the Task 4 three-driver bootstrap CI (VaR 150,859 with 95% CI [149,634, 152,369], SE 692 / +-0.91%); benchmark every aggregated figure to the Task 3 three-driver nested ground truth. Residual closure requires credentialled credit-data calibration + independent APS X2 review (tracked jointly with MR-006 / MR-008 / MR-011).
risk_register.related_standard: IA TAS M §3.6; APS X2 §3; SOA ASOP 56 §3.5; ASOP 25 §3.3
risk_register.notes: Phase 22 Task 4 re-ran the seven-driver aggregation with the Task 3 G-LIQX-CALIBRATED liquidity exposure notional and 7x7 liquidity couplings - the LAST liquidity-related educational placeholders at aggregation level are now replaced by reproducible, gate-validated values. Liquidity standalone SCR moved 63.3 -> 45.1 under the calibrated notional (30000 -> 22000) and couplings. Driver coverage remains complete (all seven documented drivers aggregate). REMAINING residual is data quality, not coverage or wiring: educational-proxy market data pending credentialled sources + independent APS X2 review.

risk_register.title: Two-factor rates market consistency not fully calibrated
risk_register.description: The G2++ rates driver now has analytic zero-coupon and bond-option formulas, but parameters are educational until calibrated to a swaption volatility surface and validated through martingale and capital-impact gates.
risk_register.mitigation: Complete Phase 20 Tasks 2-4: swaption-surface calibration, market-consistency martingale gate, and capital re-aggregation/tail diagnostics with independent review.
risk_register.related_standard: SOA ASOP 56 section 3.5; IA TAS M section 3.6
risk_register.notes: Phase 20 Task 3 added the G-MART market-consistency gate: the deflated-asset / forward martingale identities hold under Q within Monte-Carlo tolerance for the HW1F (exact dynamics) and G2++ rate drivers, GBM equity, and FX (covered interest parity). G-MART PASS (worst 1.22 sigma; max rel err 3.90e-04 over 40000 paths). A documented diagnostic shows the EDUCATIONAL monthly-Euler HW1F simulate() carries a ~7% martingale bias vs the exact dynamics (use exact simulation for market-consistent work). Residual: capital re-aggregation with the 2F driver (Task 4), UI surfacing (Task 5), recalibration to a validated market surface, and independent review remain pending.

risk_register.title: Management-action omission in liability / capital model
risk_register.description: Policyholder options (dynamic lapse) are modelled but insurer management actions are not: reversionary-bonus participation is static, so TVOG and tail capital are biased upward relative to a realistic with-management-action basis (ASOP 56 3.1.3 asymmetry; Solvency II Art. 23).
risk_register.mitigation: Phase 23 Task 3: governed dynamic reversionary-bonus participation cut entering the nested ground truth and the proxy basis, validated OOS at fixed pre-registered gates.
risk_register.notes: Phase 23 Task 4: management-action effect quantified at the AGGREGATION level: nested SCR 48707 -> 33118 (-32.0%); tail-matched t-copula 46756 -> 25653; copula structure rank-invariant (df unchanged 2.9451). Residual: placeholder parameters, outer-node approximation, fixed A_ref proxy.
